# UC-23.8.6 · Working Capital and Cash Conversion Cycle

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Business, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch how long money stays tied up in stock, what customers still owe, and what we owe suppliers, so we notice when the full cash round-trip stretches longer than finance expected last quarter.*

---

## Description

Computes **DIO**, **DSO**, **DPO**, and **CCC** from ERP subledger and G/L extracts ingested through **DB Connect**, using **COGS** in the denominator for **DIO** and **DPO**, **net sales** (or net credit sales if you filter cash and returns) for **DSO**, and an explicit **days_in_period** factor. The search expects point-in-time inventory valuation, open **AR** and **AP** balances, and rolling **ACDOCA** (or equivalent) sums for cost and revenue accounts—aligned to your chart of accounts ranges—not a single revenue field reused for every ratio.

## Value

Treasury and the **CFO** office see whether cash is tied up longer in inventory and receivables than payables extend, which directly feeds liquidity planning, covenant conversations, and working-capital programmes. A disciplined daily or weekly **CCC** trend—reconciled to the **ERP** aging lists and the finance **flash**—surfaces policy drifts (terms, collections, stock builds) before the quarterly board pack is printed, and quantifies how many days of cash a five-day cycle improvement typically releases at your revenue scale. At roughly ten billion dollars of annual sales, a five-day **CCC** improvement releases on the order of a hundred thirty million dollars in working capital—drop your own top-line into the runbook when you brief leadership.

## Implementation

(1) Land **MBEW**/**BSID**/**BSIK**/**ACDOCA** (or Oracle/NetSuite analogues) via **DB Connect** with stable `_time` for snapshot windows and **HSL**/**DMBTR** for local-currency math. (2) Publish a small **lookup** for **RACCT** ranges, `days_in_period`, and company code so denominators stay audit-traceable. (3) Schedule after the nightly **ERP** extract and compare to treasury’s same-day numbers before alerting. (4) Alert when **CCC** moves more than five days versus a twenty-day moving baseline **and** the gap reproduces in the **ERP** aging reports.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Heavy Forwarder or **DB Connect** execution host with outbound JDBC to **SAP HANA**, Oracle, SQL Server, or Postgres holding **ERP** tables; follow `splunkVersions` on search heads.
- Read-only database user with **SELECT** on **MBEW**/**MARD** views (or your published valuation view), **BSID**/**BSIK** for open items, and **ACDOCA** (or **BKPF**/**BSEG** if you remain on legacy **ECC**). Map **HSL**/**DMBTR** for local **treasury** reporting; use **KSL** only when group **currency** is the policy.
- **Lookup** `ccc_racct_policy.csv` with company code, low/high **RACCT** strings for **COGS** and revenue, optional `BLART` allowlist for **net sales**, and `days_in_period` (30/90/365). Keep the file under change control.
- **Identity** store for **DB Connect** with secret rotation; separate service account from **SAP** dialog users.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
**Path A (primary — JDBC to **S/4HANA**):** Install **Splunk DB Connect** from Splunkbase 2686 (`splunk_app_db_connect`). Create a **Connection** with the **SAP** **HANA** JDBC URL, `com.sap.db.jdbc.Driver`, and `ngdbc.jar` on the driver path. Add **Data Inputs** that run **SQL** you agree with **FI**/**CO**:
- **Inventory snapshot:** `SELECT` plant, material, **SALK3**, **LBKUM**, **VPRSV**, **BWKEY**, `WERKS`, `MATNR` from **MBEW** (or a secured view) with **WHERE** clauses that match your **DIO** policy (include or exclude **in-transit** stock per **Step 5**).
- **Open AR:** `SELECT` from **BSID** where **AUGBL** is initial (open item) and **BUKRS** in (:company_set); project **DMBTR**, **WRBTR**, **ZFBDT**, **ZTERM**, **UMSKZ**, **KUNNR**.
- **Open AP:** `SELECT` from **BSIK** with the same open-item rule; project **LIFNR**, **DMBTR**, **WRBTR**.
- **ACDOCA** pull for the rolling cost and sales window: filter **RACCT** to **COGS** and revenue ranges, **RBUKRS**, **GJAHR**, **POPER**, **BLART**, **HSL**, **KSL**, **WSL** — push down `WHERE` **GJAHR** / **POPER** in **SQL** to limit bytes.
Set each input’s **sourcetype** explicitly (examples: `dbx:sap_mbew_wc`, `dbx:sap_bsid_open`, `dbx:sap_bsik_open`, `dbx:sap_acdoca_gl`). **DB Connect** emits `dbx:<name>` by **convention**; the **actual** string is whatever you set on that **Data Input**—document it in the runbook and keep the sample SPL in sync.

**Path B (warehouse or **Oracle**/**NetSuite**):** Point **DB Connect** at an **EDW** fact table that already matches **ERP** close. For **Oracle**, map **MTL**/**CST** tables to `inventory_value`, **AR_PAYMENT_SCHEDULES_ALL** to open **AR**, **AP** schedules to open **AP**, and **GL_JE_LINES** to **COGS** and revenue with the same **day** bucketing. For **NetSuite**, filter `transaction` types for **CustInvc**, **VendBill**, and **COGS** lines. Expect longer lag than **Path A**; label dashboards when freshness exceeds **SLA**. When the **warehouse** **lags** **S/4** by **twenty**-**four** **hours**, stamp **extract** **timestamps** on each **row** so **treasury** knows whether **CCC** is **T+0** or **T+1** relative to the **ERP** **aging** **screens** they **verify** **by** **hand**.

**Step 2 — Create the search and alert**
Open **Search**, paste the logic from the top-level **`spl`** field, then retarget `index=` and every `sourcetype=` to your live names. Replace `match(tostring(RACCT),"^5...")` with **lookup**-driven bounds if your chart does not follow the **SAP** template numbering. **Coalesce** **HSL** ahead of **KSL** when local **currency** is mandatory; reverse only for group roll-ups. **Join** optional `ccc_racct_policy.csv` on **RBUKRS** to pick `days_in_period` and account filters per **company code**.

**How to read the mechanics:** the first **stats** builds **inventory_value** from the latest **MBEW** snapshot. Each **appendcols** branch is a tight subsearch that returns one row so the main pipeline stays **wide**. **COGS** and **net sales** sums use different **where** clauses on the same **ACDOCA** feed so **DIO**/**DPO** share **COGS** while **DSO** uses sales—fixing the anti-pattern of reusing **revenue** for **DIO**. **daily_cogs** and **daily_sales** divide the rolling totals by **days_in_period**; **CCC** is **DIO** + **DSO** − **DPO**.

Save as a **Report** and optional **Alert** when **CCC** minus a **twenty**-event moving average of **CCC** exceeds **five** days, **or** when any component passes a **treasury** threshold from a **lookup**—encode the **logic** in **eval**, not in narrative alone.

**Step 3 — Validate**
Tie **inventory_value** to **MB03** display material valuation (or your **CFO**-signed recon spreadsheet) for two **MATNR**/**WERKS** pairs. Reconcile **ar_open_lc** to **FBL5N** / **S_ALR_87012168** and **ap_open_lc** to **FBL1N** / **S_ALR_87012078** for the same **BUKRS** and **cut-off** time, and cross-check with **Fiori** **Manage** **Customer** / **Supplier** **Line** **Items** **UI** totals when your security role allows. For **ACDOCA**, pick one **GJAHR**/**POPER** and compare **sum(HSL)** for the **RACCT** **COGS** range to **FAGLL03**; repeat for revenue. Compare the Splunk **CCC** to the **treasury** **flash**; expect differences when the **flash** is **fiscal**-period **average** and Splunk is **rolling**—document which view is **covenant**-grade. **Cohort** **tip:** if **DPO** should reflect **purchases** not **COGS**, work with **procurement** to add a **third** **ACDOCA** **slice** (or **PO**-**informed** **view**) so **DPO** matches **AP**-**turnover** **definitions** your **lenders** already **recognize**.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**
Publish a **Dashboard Studio** row of single values with **token**-selectable `BUKRS` and `days_in_period`. Add a footnote for **data freshness** (last **DB** **extract**). Route **alerts** to **treasury** and **FP&A** with links to the saved **search**; reserve paging for sustained breaches **across** two business days. Archive weekly PDF snapshots if **lenders** need evidence.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**
- **Inventory valuation method drift** — if **DIO** jumps right after a **cutover**, check whether **MBEW**/**VPRSV** moved from **S** to **V** (or category-level cost **reprofile**). **Standard** cost holds steady; **moving average** revalues on every receipt, changing **SALK3** without physical movement.
- **Multi-currency translation** — when **HSL** and **KSL** diverge, confirm **DB** **SQL** and **ACDOCA** both use the **TCURR** rate type the **treasury** policy names (for example **M** at **period** end versus **daily** **spot**). A five-to-fifteen percent swing in ratios can be **FX** only. When **HSL** **values** are **missing** but **WSL** is still populated, suspect incomplete **translation** **jobs** before you blame **collections**.
- **Inter-company eliminations** — **BSID**/**BSIK** may still carry **KUNNR**/**LIFNR** pairs that **net** in **group** reporting. Maintain a **lookup** of **IC** **partners** (from **KNA1** **group** fields or **T880**) and add `NOT` **filters** when **consolidated** **CCC** is the question.
- **Period-end timing** — **open items** are true only for the **snapshot** instant. **Close** the **index** **time** **window** to the **DB** **extract** that matches the **board** **freeze**; **do not** mix **now()**-live **clears** into a **closed** **GJAHR**/**POPER** view.
- **DSO** **denominator** **misalignment** — if **AR** is in **WRBTR** (document **currency**) but **ACDOCA** **sales** are **HSL** (local), **align** to **DMBTR**/**HSL** on both sides before **division**.
- **Write-offs** vs **collections** — when open **AR** falls because of **AB**-style **write-off** **BLART** rather than **payment**, **DSO** improves on paper. Join **BSAD** cleared lines and exclude non-cash **clearing** **types** your **controller** names.
- **days_in_period** **misalignment** — a **thirty**-day **convention** in a **thirty**-one-**day** month understates day metrics by roughly **one**-**ninth** of a day each time; either use **actual** **days** in month from a **lookup** or print the **assumption** on the **dashboard**.
- **In-transit** **inventory** — **movement** **types** like **641**/**643** can park value outside **MARD** on-hand. Decide with **SCM** whether **in-transit** is **in** or **out** of **DIO** and add **SQL** **filters** accordingly.
- **Down payments and special G/L lines** — **UMSKZ** down-payment rows in **BSID** can show **negative** **buckets** that **mask** true **overdue** risk. **Split** a **second** **panel** for **special** **indicators** or **exclude** from the headline **DSO** per **policy**.

- **Aged** vs **gross** **AR** for **DSO** — some **shops** use **gross** **open** **AR**; others use only **past**-**due** lines. Mismatching the **CFO** **definition** causes endless **arguing**; mirror the **aging** **report** **logic** in **SQL** or **eval** **filters**.

**Reconciliation** **addenda:** **Quarterly** **board** **packs** often show a **fiscal**-**period** **average** **CCC**, while this **search** uses a **rolling** **window**—run a **second** **saved** **report** grouped by **GJAHR**/**POPER** when you need **director**-**meeting** **parity**. **Bank** **covenant** **workbooks** may define **DSO** as **(open** **AR** **/** **quarterly** **revenue**) **×** **90** instead of **rolling** **thirty**-**day** **sales**; clone the **pipeline** with that **denominator** when **waiver** **testing** is the **audience**. If **inventory** comes from **MATDOC** instead of **MSEG**, keep **movement** **type** **filters** aligned with **finance** on **in**-**transit** **stock**. **Oracle** shops should confirm **CST_ITEM_COSTS** **cost** **type** matches the **COGS** **map** in **GL**; **NetSuite** deployments need **currency** on **transaction** **headers** consistent with **local** **ledger** **postings**.


## SPL

```spl
index=business sourcetype="dbx:sap_mbew_wc" earliest=-1d@d latest=now()
| stats sum(eval(coalesce(SALK3_val,tonumber(SALK3),0))) as inventory_value
| appendcols [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:sap_bsid_open" earliest=-1d@d latest=now()
    | where (isnull(AUGBL) OR AUGBL="")
    | stats sum(eval(coalesce(tonumber(DMBTR),tonumber(WRBTR),0))) as ar_open_lc
]
| appendcols [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:sap_bsik_open" earliest=-1d@d latest=now()
    | where (isnull(AUGBL) OR AUGBL="")
    | stats sum(eval(coalesce(tonumber(DMBTR),tonumber(WRBTR),0))) as ap_open_lc
]
| appendcols [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:sap_acdoca_gl" earliest=-30d@d latest=now()
    | where match(tostring(RACCT),"^5[0-9]{5,7}$")
    | stats sum(eval(coalesce(tonumber(HSL),tonumber(KSL),0))) as cogs_30d_lc
]
| appendcols [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:sap_acdoca_gl" earliest=-30d@d latest=now()
    | where match(tostring(RACCT),"^4[0-9]{5,7}$") AND BLART IN ("RV","RE","DZ")
    | stats sum(eval(coalesce(tonumber(HSL),tonumber(KSL),0))) as net_sales_30d_lc
]
| eval days_in_period=30
| eval daily_cogs=if(cogs_30d_lc>0, cogs_30d_lc/days_in_period, null())
| eval daily_sales=if(net_sales_30d_lc>0, net_sales_30d_lc/days_in_period, null())
| eval dio=if(isnotnull(daily_cogs) AND daily_cogs!=0, round(inventory_value/daily_cogs,0), null())
| eval dso=if(isnotnull(daily_sales) AND daily_sales!=0, round(ar_open_lc/daily_sales,0), null())
| eval dpo=if(isnotnull(daily_cogs) AND daily_cogs!=0, round(ap_open_lc/daily_cogs,0), null())
| eval ccc=if(isnotnull(dio) AND isnotnull(dso) AND isnotnull(dpo), round(dio + dso - dpo,0), null())
| table inventory_value, ar_open_lc, ap_open_lc, cogs_30d_lc, net_sales_30d_lc, dio, dso, dpo, ccc
```

## Visualization

Single values for **CCC**, **DIO**, **DSO**, **DPO** with prior-period deltas; waterfall of **DIO** + **DSO** − **DPO**; line chart of **CCC** versus the moving average; table of daily snapshot history with `BUKRS` or business unit; optional trellis by **WERKS** (plant) when inventory is plant-specific.

## Known False Positives

Rolling **CCC** can improve when finance reclassifies consignment stock between owner and vendor accounts even though pallets never moved, which is a legitimate policy change rather than a collections win. A sharp **DSO** decline may appear after intercompany netting removes mirror **AR** rows in one company code while revenue still posts at the selling entity, so the ratio looks cleaner while external cash is unchanged. Retail **DIO** often rises before peak season on planned **inventory** prebuild; compare the same retail week year over year before paging **treasury**. **Covenant** definitions that use ninety-day revenue in the denominator while this search uses thirty will disagree by design—reconcile to the lender worksheet, not to the board slide. Duplicate-payment recovery sweeps can temporarily inflate **open AP** until reversal documents post, stretching **DPO** for a few days without a true supplier-term change. **FX** revaluation of **BSID**/**BSIK** balances at month-end can move **DSO**/**DPO** several points with no operational shift if **HSL** and **ACDOCA** use different rate types. A **flash** that freezes at **period close** while Splunk keeps ingesting live clears will diverge after the close **window**—expected, not a parser bug. One-time **write-offs** hitting **AR** move open balance and can look like faster collection unless you exclude those **BLART** paths. **Standard** versus **moving-average** cost flips on a **material** category change **average** inventory **value** without a physical count shift, which changes **DIO** even when foot traffic is flat.
